steps.narrate: It uncoiled from the canopy like something the forest had been hiding from itself.
Too many limbs. Too few joints. A throat that opened the wrong way.

steps.speaker: Elia
steps.text: It's hollow. There's no person left in it. You can hit it as hard as you need to.

choice.text: Burn the warmth in your hand. Channel it through the blade.

steps.narrate: [ Filler — choice always jumps. ]
He stepped in. The blade went where it always went — clean, efficient, taught long ago by hands he could no longer name.
The thing screamed without using its mouth.
A grey limb whipped down where his head had been a moment before. He felt the pressure of it as much as he saw it.

steps.speaker: Elia
steps.text: It's faster than the last one!

steps.narrate: First wound: shallow. Survival, not victory.
But it was bleeding now — if grey smoke pouring out of a wound counted as bleeding.
Warm light through a window. A room he could not place. He held it in his hand and let it go.
The blade caught fire — not heat, but the *idea* of heat, golden and impossible.
It cleaved through the beast's torso like through cloth.

steps.speaker: Arrel
steps.text: It was warmth I'll never feel again.

steps.narrate: The beast staggered, half its weight gone in one strike.
He held the blade low and let it come.
The thing moved in waves — limbs not striking but *flowing*, looking for a way through his guard.
He saw the pattern. Three sweeps high, one low. A rhythm older than the creature, older than the forest.
When the low strike came, he stepped through it and put the sword between two of its ribs.
It was. The beast retreated, smoke leaking, cautious now.
[ === Round 2 starts at index 23 === ]
The beast did not die from one wound. It *learned* from it.
It widened. The hollow at its center opened, and from inside, more grey — older grey, the kind that smelled like other people's losses.

choice.text: Burn the smell of forest after rain. Make every cut count.
Use what you read. Counter its rhythm.

steps.narrate: [ Filler. ]
He moved faster than was wise. The beast's limb caught his shoulder, and pain bloomed bright and clean.
But the blade was already through it. Through it. Through it.
The smell of wet earth after rainfall. Where, when — gone.
Every strike found its mark. The beast could not turn away from him.
Its grey opened, opened, until there was nothing left for it to be.
He moved as he had read the beast move — not against its rhythm, but *with* it.
When its limb came high, he was already low. When the low strike opened, he was already past it.
The killing cut went where the rhythm wanted it to go, and the beast had no answer.

steps.speaker: Arrel
steps.text: It didn't have one to learn.

steps.narrate: [ === Endgame at 47 === ]
What remained of the beast collapsed into a stain of grey ash that the wind took, slowly, like the wind was trying to be respectful about it.
He stood over it, breathing harder than he wanted Elia to see.

steps.speaker: Elia
steps.text: You're hurt. And you burned for it.

steps.narrate: He didn't answer.

steps.speaker: Elia
steps.text: I just want to know what was worth it.

steps.speaker: Arrel
steps.text: Standing here. Talking to you. That was worth it.

steps.narrate: She looked at him a long moment. Then she turned, and they walked on.
